advice: Use insecticide-treated mosquito nets every night. Seek testing immediately if fever persists beyond 24 hours. Avoid stagnant water near your home.
Drink only boiled or treated water. Wash hands frequently with soap. Avoid raw or undercooked foods from unknown sources.
Eliminate standing water where mosquitoes breed. Use repellent and wear long sleeves. Seek care immediately for high fever with severe headache.
Typhoid spreads through contaminated food and water. Vaccination is available. Practice strict hand hygiene before eating.
Annual flu vaccine is the best protection. Rest, hydrate, and isolate if symptomatic. High-risk individuals should seek care early.
Ensure children are vaccinated (MMR). Isolate confirmed cases immediately. Report suspected cases to local health authorities.
